reviews.review_text: Very fun, boardgame-like game. Definitely meant for multiplayer, likely would not be much fun solo but is fantastic with a group.
Very fun and interesting game for someone who usually doesn't play turn-based games, 25 hours in and I'm still attempting to kill the kings murderer. I Suggest this game
The game's rules are poorly written. Picking up a side quest and not being able to abandon it is ridiculous.
Its a good coop game with friends and it runs well on my pc
Have a lot of bug, game always crash, connection also unstable. For the playability of this game, it is also boring and dull.
Great game fun combat and rewards gambling Blacksmith forever my king

its so silly, whimsical, and fun. one of the few games i would play hours on end for, and its my family's favorite game to play together. i managed to convert their partners into the game too, alas my army of ftk party members are expanding so i can conquer that damn jungle...
Love this game, I have it on xbox and switch as well. Always fun with friends or solo, lots of replayability.
It's great!  Nice and easy co-op play to jump into and jump out!

This is a good solo game for about 30-40 hours if you enjoy multiple playthrough value. It does not get updates as of 2026 there's the 2nd for the king 2 game out now but honestly it just feels ok nothing special. buy this on sale and its worth the money for sure.
I literally have 32.5 hours in this game and I lose at the end every single time.... but I will never stop

It could be that I've played too much Darkest Dungeon but the whole gameplay is super clunky, healing feels quite rare and costly, I also don't like the periodic difficulty increases that punish you for wanting to explore... I thought I'd really like this game so this is super disappointing.

Yes Yes and Yes! Grab this game when on sale, I had lot of fun and bought another copy for my friend. I mostly play with my friend controlling each character, the mechanic really straight foward kinda confusing at first until you discover by yourself. Beside u can only play coop for 3 players max the game still have contents inside with their own unique difficulties, planning your turn really important because the longer u skip the goal the harder its get, its a win and lose situation sometimes and saving your gold to buy gear u looking for is such a bless for your playthrough. Get this game!
fun to play with friends I really enjoy it. Poor persons Baldurs Gate 3 lol.

insane game for someone who wants dungeons and dragons kind of feel, very well made and balanced me and my friend are still figuring out new things even after 8 hours of play, i feel like we didnt cover 30% of the first map or world
Its a good game to relax and enjoy with your friends me and my friends had some funny moments while playing this.

Fun boardgame like feeling game to play one weekends on run longs with 2 other friends or even solo is fun. Such well done boardgame like combat and campaign! It does get hard as you get to later part of the game but with the right strategy and items and a little bit of rng luck, you can overcome hard enemies. Really had a blast playing with friends and even solo. Combat was satisfying and the feeling of getting random items after every combat feels so rewarding and addicting, making each players get stronger and stronger. Recommend it buying at full price even

The initial learning phase and the amount of things that pop up that have heavy RNG based outcomes are big, so if you are a fan of big risk big rewards kind of gameplay it can be fun. And if, like me, you don't want that, there are pretty consistent ways to ignore most of the RNG dependent parts of the game and build up your roster to consistently do great.

This game is a very good game. Its a roguelike board game that is very challenging and will require you to strategize, and to use every resource to your advantage, including time. unlike most other games the game is actually time sensitive where the world ending threat will cause a game over if you take too long to complete objectives(However, the game presents optional side quest objectives that if completed will give you extra time to complete the rest of the main objectives so if you accidently waste too much time its possible to recover. so its a mechanic that's present, but does its best to not be annoying, and the speed can be customized to your liking) 

However, this game is not an instant buy for a few reasons for some people, as I will state below.

This game is incredibly difficult, so much so that you are expected to fail on your first few attempts, The game even has an in-game pop-up message that will appear when you load the game indicating this to you. and if you fail, you have to start the campaign all the way over from the beginning, since this game is a roguelike so perma-death is present here. So if you don't like difficult games then this isn't the game for you. However, the game does have customizable difficulty settings so you can make the game easier or harder depending on what difficulty you feel like playing on. When you fail, you earn currency(lore points) to use in the lore store. In the lore store you can buy new classes, friendly encounters, and equipment from the store by earning currency in your campaign runs that will help make your future runs easier. These upgrades are permanent, and will persist for all future campaigns.

Secondly, The game has a slight design flaw that can make it feel awkward to play alone. The game was designed as a 3 player board game, so while it is technically possible to play it solo it can feel awkward as you end up controlling all 3 player characters, so it ends up being you playing as 3 players. Normally its 1 player character per player, so if you play solo you end up being all 3 player characters. however, this isn't really a huge issue, its just something to keep in mind if you don't like that sort of thing. However the main reason I say this is a design flaw is because the game is 3 player. so if you try playing multiplayer with 1 other person and end up trying to play 2 player, it wont really work as it will be one person controlling 2 characters while the other only controls one character. So your friend has to end up watching you take 2 turns and deal with 2 characters while they only get one turn and one character, which can feel unfair, especially since each character doesn't get their own gold reward, gold is spilt among the 3 characters, so you end up getting more gold than your friend does. so really this game is either 1 player or 3 player due to the weird way it was designed. However, I will say this. the game is possible to play with only 2 player characters, so it is possible for each person to control only 1 character. however the game was balanced around 3 players, not 2. so if you decide to do this, it will make the game considerably more difficult to beat.

These are just very minor issues though that don't impact the quality of the game all that much but still are worth mentioning, if you can ignore these issues and you're a person that likes difficult games that push you to your limits and make you think strategically then this game is for you. This game is a very good game that I have been playing solo and with 2 of my friends for a few years. Oh, and if you're wondering why my hour count is so low, that's because I played it on the PS4 before I ended up switching to steam, so that's why my hour count is so low despite me saying that I've played for a few years.
Definitely the best virtual, turn-based RPG I have played. No other game I played translates the tabletop RPG energy to your computer as For The King does. 10/10, especially with buddies.

This turn-based strategy game invites to embark on a cooperative adventure in a beautifully crafted, procedurally generated world. With elements of RPG and board games, it offers an engaging blend of exploration, combat, and resource management. You can choose from various characters, each with unique abilities, and work together to overcome challenges. The game's difficulty and permadeath mechanics add a layer of tension, making every decision count. While the replayability is high due to its randomized elements, you may find the strategic depth can become repetitive over time. Overall, it's an enjoyable experience for fans of tactical gameplay.

I have played this game for so many years, both single-player and with friends, local and online multiplayer. It’s a great gateway game for friends to grasp ttrpg mechanics, it’s a cut tribute for friends who already love ttrpgs, and it’s a joy for roguelike lovers. The art direction is so delightful, especially the “leaking triangles” particle animations for wounded characters and the ragdoll physics and bone launching that correspond to the weapons that deal death blows. The difficulty settings, rng, and stat+class mechanics are a great way to keep things fresh. The endless dungeon mode is also fun, whittling away overworld mechanics for pure dungeon crawling, though ultimately I prefer the regular campaign modes. The DLC was a great addition!

I'm really really really REALLY tired of the "cult of difficult" and the really really really REALLY ♥♥♥♥♥♥ game design idea of making healing rare and taking massive difficult damage easy. It's nasty and psychologically manipulative and needs to go away. "For the King" is a robustly built mix of all traditional D&D-inspired elements without any novelty, but crudely infected with the "cult of difficult" and a ♥♥♥♥ UI.
If you're a masochist with a major gambling addiction, then you'll probably love torturing yourself with this I guess.
been playing for years, always fun to come back to.
